overview: The Research Development and Innovation (RDI) Scheme is a flagship initiative under the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India, designed to accelerate investment in India's R&D and Innovation ecosystem. Approved by the Union Cabinet on 1 July 2025 and launched by the Hon'ble Prime Minister on 3 November 2025, the scheme aims to catalyse private sector investment in R&D with a total outlay of ₹1 lakh crore over six years, including ₹20,000 crore in FY 2025-26. It supports private sector enterprises, startups, and industries working in sunrise and strategic sectors to transform ideas into globally competitive technologies and products, with a focus on high-TRL projects (TRL 4 and above), acquisition of critical technologies, and creation of Deep-Tech Funds.
objectives: Encourage the private sector to scale up RDI in sunrise domains and in other sectors relevant for economic security, strategic purpose and self-reliance
Finance transformative projects at higher levels of Technology Readiness Level (TRL 4 and above)
Support the acquisition of technologies which are critical or of high strategic importance
Facilitate the establishment of a dedicated Deep-Tech Fund of Funds
eligibility: Eligible Technology Entities (ETEs) include any legal entity engaged in developing Research, Development & Innovation (RDI)-intensive technology (Technology Readiness Level 4 and above), such as companies registered under the Companies Act, 2013, partnerships under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, limited liability partnerships under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008, and startups as defined in the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade Notification G.S.R. 127(E) dated 19 February 2019, as may be modified from time to time. Entities must be registered in India under Indian laws and under control of resident Indian citizens, retain ownership of IPR registered in India under Indian laws/rules, and comply with GoI directions on security, export control, and other strategic concerns.
benefits: The scheme provides long-term, innovation-driven financing through low-interest long-term loans (typically unsecured), equity financing (especially for startups and early-stage deep-tech ventures), and contributions to Deep-Tech Fund of Funds (FoF) or other ROI-oriented FoFs aligned with national innovation priorities. It does not provide grants or short-term loans. Financing is available up to 50% of the total assessed project cost, with the remaining cost to be met by the project proponent through self-financing or commercial sources. In exceptional cases or strategic sectors, this limit may be relaxed with EGoS approval. The scheme also supports patient capital at low rates to survive failure risk and sustain R&D’s extended gestation periods.
financial_support: The RDI Fund provides financing up to 50% of the total assessed project cost, with the remaining 50% to be sourced by the project proponent from self-financing or commercial sources. In exceptional cases or strategic sectors, this limit may be relaxed with EGoS approval. Financing modes include low-interest long-term loans (typically unsecured), equity financing (especially for startups), and contributions to Deep-Tech Fund of Funds (FoF). The scheme does not provide grants or short-term loans. Funding is routed through Second Level Fund Managers (SLFMs) such as Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs), Development Finance Institutions (DFIs), Non-Banking Finance Companies (NBFCs), and Focused Research Organisations (FROs), which invest in Eligible Technology Entities (ETEs) advancing RDI-intensive technologies.
application_process: 1. Register on the RDIF Management Portal at www.rdifund.anrf.gov.in. 2. Submit application via the portal’s workflows by the prescribed deadline. 3. Provide information and supporting documents related to Quality criteria in Digital Sealed Cover 1. 4. Provide undertakings related to Cost criteria in Digital Sealed Cover 2. 5. Applications are evaluated using the Quality and Cost Based Selection (QCBS) method with 80% weightage to Quality criteria and 20% to Cost criteria. 6. Quality criteria assess General Competence, R&D and RDI-Intensive Technology Focus, and ‘Crowd In’ of private capital. 7. Cost criteria for AIFs focus on management fees and carried interest; for Loan modality, on net interest margins and management fees. 8. Selected SLFMs must invest RDIF funds only in companies including startups advancing RDI-intensive technologies at TRL 4 and above, working in RDIF Priority Sectors, and in accordance with Funding Guidelines.
required_documents: Certificate of Registration for the current Fund from SEBI under Regulation 6 of SEBI (Alternative Investment Funds) Regulations, 2012
Names and details of Trustees (if Fund corpus is a Scheme of a Trust)
Names and details of Directors, with DIN for Investment Manager/Asset Management Company or LLP
GST Registration Certificate and PAN Card for Investment Manager/Asset Management Company or LLP
Undertaking that the Applicant has no conflict of interest regarding RDIF decision-making
Private Placement Memorandum of Fund submitted to SEBI
Details on funds raised so far, supported by signed Contribution Agreements

Trustee/Investment Manager Profile Briefs with examples of success in managing teams, leading deals/exits, driving scale-up, emerging technologies, product management, finance/investment, mentoring startups, building processes for performance/risk management, raising third-party capital, deal-sourcing/due diligence, portfolio management, exits, relevant priority sectors in India, TRLs from Level 4 onward, and venture development stages
Relevant chapters in Private Placement Memorandum (e.g., Trustee or Investment Manager profiles)

Curriculum Vitae/Biodata containing names and experience profiles of Partners, Investment Committee Members, Advisory Board Members, and number of funds with which individual has been associated (with exit details)
Number of years Fund Management Team has worked together
Manpower Details (Category, Number of personnel, Key expertise/role)
Tabulated Portfolio Summary of investees of each Fund (including entry/exit valuations, Net IRR, MOIC, DPIC, Yield on Loans/Portfolio Yield for debt AIFs, Vintage Year Performance)
Investment Thesis Note or Private Placement Memorandum showing technological focus, industry sector focus, market focus, technology maturity ranges (TRLs), and venture development stage
Documentation of prior investments in RDI-intensive technology firms (with profiles on startup investments) and exits from RDI-intensive firms (with exit route details)
Details on investment sizes at previous Funds (tabulated summaries, average and modal value of investments)
Percentage of contributions invested across previous Funds (written statement)
Contribution Agreement from other Private Contributors and Information Brief on private contribution from LPs
Written undertaking specifying proposed Management Fee (as % of aggregate Capital Commitment or outstanding Investments) and Carried Interest rate with justification
Preferred Funding Mode (Mode 1/2/3), Internal Rate of Return (IRR) offered, Distribution Priority for RDIF (Higher/Pari-passu/Lower), Fund Tenure (Years)

Technology Focus, Sector Focus (multi-select: Energy and Climate Action, Deep Technologies, AI and Applications, Biotechnology/Pharma/Medical Devices, Digital Economy/Agriculture, Others), Target Value for First Close, Target Value for Final Close, Private Contribution (% of Aggregate Capital), RDIF Contribution Sought (INR), RDIF Contribution sought as % of Aggregate Capital

Undertakings Required for Authorised Person and Company Secretary (including Name of Applicant Company, Complete postal address, Address for correspondence, Name of Company Secretary certifying documents, Names and passport numbers of authorised signatories, Name of Authorised Person, designation, address, Telephone/Fax numbers/email address, specimen signature counter-signed by authorised signatory, Power of Attorney by Board Resolution)

Written undertaking stating: 'The SLFM shall ensure that there is no conflict of interest in the choice of specific projects for financing, given the likelihood of domain experts on its Investment Committee being investors in ideas and companies'
deadlines: Applications must be submitted on the RDIF Management Portal at www.rdifund.anrf.gov.in by 1700 hours IST on 31 January 2026.
implementing_agency: Department of Science & Technology (DST), Ministry of Science & Technology
contact_details: Email: rdi-fund@anrf.gov.in | RDI Cell - DST Room No. 7A, Old S&T Building, Technology Bhawan | RDIF Unit - ANRF Room No. 8, 4th Floor, Block II, Technology Bhawan, New Mehrauli Road, New Delhi - 110 016
caveats: The scheme does not provide grants or short-term loans.

Financing is limited to 50% of total project cost, with the remaining 50% to be sourced by the project proponent (self or commercial sources), except in exceptional cases or strategic sectors with EGoS approval.
RDIF funds must be used exclusively to support RDI-intensive technologies (TRL 4 and above) in RDIF Priority Sectors.
SLFMs must invest all RDIF-contributed funds plus matching private capital only in Eligible Technology Entities focusing on RDI-intensive technologies and RDIF Priority Sectors.
Government entities and CPSEs are eligible only if partnering with the private sector on strategic projects.
RDIF will not finance RDI by government entities and central public sector enterprises (CPSEs) unless involved in strategic projects with private sector.
